Title: Malaysia targets higher local vehicle content, warns against import dependence
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is pushing for higher local content in vehicle manufacturing as the government seeks to strengthen domestic suppliers and reduce the growing dependence on imported vehicles and components.
Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said investors must play a bigger role in developing Malaysia's industrial ecosystem rather than simply selling vehicles.
"Investors should not come merely to sell vehicles. They must be committed to building the industry in Malaysia, involving local SMEs and helping to develop the nation's supply chain."
Johari said local content utilisation will be increased progressively from around 10 per cent to as much as 40 per cent as the industry's capabilities improve.
"Each vehicle consists of around 25-30 major components. Not every component needs to be manufactured locally, but those that are suitable should be produced by local companies to strengthen the supporting industry," he said at the launch of MCE Auto Hub here today.
Johari said Malaysia's automotive components industry has expanded significantly, with its value rising from about RM13 billion in 2020 to RM33.8 billion in 2025.
However, he cautioned that the country's motor vehicle trade deficit, including parts and components, had also widened sharply over the same period.
"Without a strategic and forward-looking approach, there is a real risk that the domestic market will become increasingly dominated by imported products, placing greater pressure on the local supplier ecosystem."
Johari also stressed that economic growth must translate into stronger SME development.
"However, the government believes that economic growth alone is insufficient if SMEs do not directly benefit.
"If the economy grows rapidly but SMEs record only marginal growth over the same period, they will struggle to survive, particularly when inflationary pressures are taken into account."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia, India announce BrahMos missile deal
Article:
JAKARTA: India will supply Indonesia with long-range missiles, an Indian official said on Tuesday as their leaders agreed to deepen ties in defence, critical minerals and other areas.
President Prabowo Subianto is hosting Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi in Jakarta during a three-day state visit, with a deal on the BrahMos missile system topping the agenda.
An agreement for "cooperation on BrahMos System" was struck during the visit, Indian foreign ministry spokesman Randhir Jaiswal said in a post on X.
The two countries also reached an agreement on air-to-air missile cooperation that "enhances technology collaboration and defence capabilities", he said.
Details on the deal were not disclosed, and an Indonesian defence ministry spokesman did not respond to a request for comment.
Modi touted the "growing trust" between Jakarta and New Delhi after his meeting with Prabowo, which he said underpinned cooperation in "defence, security and maritime affairs."
"Today, we reached an agreement to enhance defence exchanges, disaster management, and industrial cooperation," Modi told reporters.
The coast guards of the two countries will also work together on maritime safety and security in the Indian Ocean, Modi said.
Asia's two biggest democracies also agreed to work together on critical minerals – which Indonesia produces in abundance – and steel.
"A new partnership between our companies is beginning in the field of stainless steel and rare earth magnets," Modi said.
Prabowo said Indonesia and India viewed economic cooperation as "one of the main pillars" of bilateral relations.
He said he and Modi agreed to increase bilateral trade and accelerate negotiations on a preferential trade agreement.
They also welcomed efforts to restore the Prambanan temple, a Unesco World Heritage Site in Yogyakarta that they will visit together on Wednesday.
Modi is set to visit Australia and New Zealand after concluding his Indonesia visit, according to an Indian foreign ministry statement. --AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Muhyiddin did not interfere in Jana Wibawa process, says Tengku Zafrul
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin did not interfere in the process of awarding projects under the Jana Wibawa programme, the High Court heard today.
Former finance minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz said this while testifying as key prosecution witness in Muhyiddin's trial involving seven charges - four counts of abuse of power and three counts of money laundering linked to the programme.
Under cross-examination by Muhyiddin's lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, the witness also agreed that the accused never interfered in an evaluation process conducted by the Jana Wibawa programme Contractor Evaluation Committee.
Tengku Zafrul said the committee would conduct the assessment and subsequently make recommendations, while the final approval would be decided by the Finance Ministry.
Amer Hamzah: In the process that you described, was there any interference by the prime minister?
Tengku Zafrul: Based on my knowledge, no.
Yesterday, Tengku Zafrul testified that KCJ Engineering Sdn Bhd, a company alleged to have contributed RM800,000 to Bersatu, had initially failed the Finance Ministry's evaluation for a Jana Wibawa programme before eventually securing a RM62 million contract.
He said the engineering and construction firm later passed its second assessment and was recommended for the project following the committee's decision at a meeting on Feb 26, 2021.
Tengku Zafrul agreed with Amer Hamzah's suggestion that there was nothing suspicious about the committee's overall process.
According to Tengku Zafrul, it was a normal evaluation process that was also undergone by other companies and not only KCJ Engineering.
Tengku Zafrul also agreed with Amer Hamzah's suggestion that every meeting minute was not an instruction and acknowledged that he had received many such minutes from Muhyiddin.
He said this when referred to the document titled "Proposal for the Appointment of 54 Implementing Contractors for the Implementation of 54 Projects Under the Jana Wibawa Programme Through Direct Negotiation (Design & Build)", which also listed the names of contractor companies.
He also agreed with the lawyer's suggestion that there was no evidence presented by the prosecution showing that Muhyiddin had any personal or financial interest in the company involved.
When it was suggested that all the projects could not be implemented by a minister alone but required approval from higher authorities, the witness replied: "Yes."
According to Tengku Zafrul, there was a need to create an economic stimulus package to generate employment opportunities during the Covid-19 pandemic, and he agreed that all the projects could not be implemented by a single minister.
Tengku Zafrul also agreed that contractors were "severely affected" at the time and that assistance given to them would create a "trickle-down effect" on the economy.
"You could see the problems faced by the Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) sector, Bumiputera businesses, contractors and the entire ecosystem.
"Based on those interests and concerns, that was why we proposed Jana Wibawa," he said.
Jana Wibawa was introduced in 2020 to speed up economic recovery from the Covid-19 pandemic through infrastructure and public works projects awarded to Bumiputera contractors via direct negotiations and pre-qualified tenders.
Muhyiddin, who is Bersatu president and Pagoh MP, faces four charges of abuse of power involving RM232.5 million in alleged gratification for the party, and three counts of money laundering involving RM200 million.
The trial before Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin continues tomorrow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: My client is autistic, says lawyer of Banting school stabbing suspect
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The 15-year-old suspect in the stabbing of a female student at a secondary school in Banting, Kuala Langat, is autistic, her lawyer said.
S Vijeswari Devi, from the National Legal Aid Foundation, said her client's condition was the only information she could disclose at this stage.
"My client is autistic. That is all I can say," she said after the remand proceedings at the Telok Datok Magistrate's Court today.
The suspect was remanded for four days from today to enable further investigations into the incident.
The remand order was issued by Magistrate Maslinda Selamat.
Kuala Langat police chief, Superintendent Mohd Akmalrizal Radzi, said the remand period would allow police to gather further evidence and record statements from those involved.
"As stated earlier, the investigation is being conducted under Section 307 of the Penal Code (for attempted murder)," he said.
The suspect was brought to court in a police vehicle at 8.10am for remand proceedings, and left the court around 9am.
The incident yesterday saw a
Form Three student at a Banting secondary school
here stabbed more than 10 times in a violent attack by another student.
The 15-year-old victim sustained multiple wounds to the back and shoulders in the incident.
The suspect, also 15, had reportedly been absent from school for several months and was undergoing medical treatment prior to the attack.
Witnesses claimed that the suspect had entered the school compound through the main gate armed with a knife.
Following the attack, a teacher reportedly managed to calm the suspect near the school's prayer room before she was detained by police.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM: Petronas dividends make up only 6 pct of govt's 2026 revenue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has dismissed claims that the federal government is overly reliant on annual dividends from Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), saying they account for only about five to six per cent of total projected revenue this year.
He said the national oil company's contribution accounts for only about five to six per cent of the government's total projected revenue this year.
In a written parliamentary reply, Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the government's projected revenue for 2026 stands at RM343.1 billion, comprising RM270.4 billion in tax revenue and RM72.7 billion in non-tax revenue.
He said the projected RM20 billion dividend from Petronas this year forms part of the non-tax revenue component.
"The federal government's main source of revenue is tax revenue, which accounts for more than 70 per cent of total government revenue, while Petronas dividends represent only around five to six per cent of the total estimated federal government revenue for 2026.
"This shows that the federal government is not overly dependent on Petronas' performance," he said.
Anwar was responding to a question from Ahmad Fadhli Shaari (Pas-Pasir Mas), who asked about the government's plans to reduce fiscal dependence on Petronas after the national oil company's dividend payments declined from RM40 billion in 2023 to RM20 billion in 2026.
Anwar said fiscal management extends beyond revenue collection to include expenditure, borrowing, debt and fiscal risk management.
He said the government remains committed to reducing its dependence on petroleum-related revenue due to exposure to fluctuations in global crude oil prices.
"Petroleum-related revenue accounted for 41.3 per cent of total federal revenue in 2009, but this had been reduced to 17.5 per cent in 2025 from 28 per cent in 2022 following revenue reforms introduced from 2023."
He said petroleum-related revenue is projected to decline further to 12.5 per cent in 2026.
Anwar said the government has also formulated a Medium-Term Revenue Strategy (MTRS) to broaden the tax base and strengthen tax administration.
Among the measures implemented are the phased rollout of the e-invoicing system to improve tax administration and compliance, the expansion of the Sales and Service Tax (SST), wider excise duties on sugar-sweetened beverages, revised crude palm oil export duties, and higher excise duties on alcoholic beverages and tobacco products.
He said fiscal reforms are also being complemented by broader economic policies under the Madani Economy framework to spur economic growth, which would in turn strengthen the country's fiscal position.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Banting stabbing: Suhakam urges public not to speculate on child suspect
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) has urged the public to refrain from speculating about the stabbing of a secondary school student in Banting, Selangor.
Suhakam's Office of the Children's Commissioner (OCC) said speculation about the circumstances of the case, including the child's medical condition, disability, mental health or alleged motive, was irresponsible.
"Such speculation risks prejudicing ongoing investigations and may perpetuate harmful stereotypes and discrimination," it said in a statement.
Suhakam said media reports indicated the suspect's legal counsel had stated that the child was autistic.
"Should this information be confirmed, the OCC stresses that disability must never be used to stigmatise, vilify or incite hatred against any child.
"Every child, including children with disabilities, has the right to equal protection before the law, dignity, non-discrimination and access to appropriate support services," it said.
While the
case
is under police investigation, Suhakam said the rights, dignity and best interests of every child involved must be respected throughout the legal process.
The OCC also expressed concern over the widespread circulation of photographs, videos, personal information and unverified claims about the children involved on social media and messaging platforms.
It said such actions could cause lasting psychological harm, expose children to public stigma, interfere with investigations and affect their rehabilitation and future reintegration into society.
Suhakam reminded the public, media organisations and online users that Section 15 of the Child Act 2001 prohibited the publication or dissemination of information that could identify a child involved in proceedings or matters protected under the Act.
The prohibition includes names, photographs, videos, school details, family information, addresses or any other particulars that could reveal a child's identity.
Those found guilty of breaching the provision face a fine of up to RM10,000, imprisonment of up to five years, or both.
Suhakam said both the victim and alleged perpetrator were children and entitled to protections under Malaysian law and the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child (CRC).
The OCC said it would continue monitoring developments and engage with relevant authorities to ensure the rights and welfare of all children involved were safeguarded.
It added that the incident highlighted the need to strengthen violence prevention measures in schools through early identification of risks, accessible mental health and psychosocial support, effective child protection mechanisms, and collaboration between schools, parents and relevant agencies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Reservoir Link's unit secures sand control equipment supply and services contract from Petronas
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Reservoir Link Energy Bhd's unit, Reservoir Link Sdn Bhd, has secured a five-year contract from Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd to provide remedial sand control equipment and services.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the oil and gas services company said its unit received the letter of award on May 21. The contract will run until May 20, 2031.
The contract covers the supply of sand screens, accessories and related services for wire wrap and wire mesh screen systems, as well as sand consolidation and sand agglomeration chemicals.
Reservoir Link will also undertake planning, design, simulation, testing and analysis for sand screen designs, in addition to assembling and installing screens and related accessories for completion systems.
"The contract is expected to contribute positively towards the earnings of the company and its subsidiaries for the financial year ending June 30, 2027 and throughout the contract period," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New mosques, surau must include facilities for PwDs, senior citizens
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: All new mosque and surau developments nationwide must incorporate facilities and infrastructure for persons with disabilities (PwDs) and senior citizens to ensure places of worship are more inclusive and accessible.
Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Marhamah Rosli said the requirement was in line with existing legal frameworks, including the Persons with Disabilities Act 2008, the Street, Drainage and Building Act 1974, the Uniform Building By-Laws 1984 and universal design guidelines.
She said most mosques and surau registered under the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (Jawi) already provide basic facilities for senior citizens and PwDs.
However, she said the government was prioritising efforts to upgrade these facilities through existing funding mechanisms, including maintenance allocations, mosque funds, community contributions and financial assistance coordinated by the respective state religious authorities.
"Some mosques and surau have already provided comprehensive facilities, including designated parking bays, accessible ablution areas and toilets, dedicated pathways, prayer chairs, Braille copies of the Quran, wheelchairs and automated external defibrillators (AEDs).
"However, the government will continue to enhance these facilities in stages, in line with projections by the Department of Statistics Malaysia that Malaysia is expected to become an ageing nation by 2030," she said in the Dewan Rakyat today.
She was responding to a question from Aminolhuda Hassan (PH-Sri Gading), who asked about efforts to ensure mosques and surau are more accessible to senior citizens and PwDs.
Marhamah said the government had introduced the Malaysian Mosque Rating (SPMM) through strategic cooperation between the Federal and state governments to encourage mosque management committees to continue improving inclusive infrastructure.
She said one of the assessment components under the standard was the provision of facilities that are friendly to senior citizens and PwDs, encouraging mosque management committees to enhance infrastructure that is inclusive, safe and accessible for all worshippers.
She added that the initiative was further supported by the Muslim PwD Religious Agencies Action Plan 2024-2028, which focuses on strengthening the capacity of mosque communities while promoting greater participation of the PwD community as strategic partners.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three terrace houses, van damaged and resident suffers burns in Penang fire
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Three terrace houses and a van were damaged in a fire at Taman Hijau 2 here today, leaving one resident with burns.
Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations John Sagun Francis said the department received an emergency call at 2.43pm.
The first fire engine from the Jalan Perak Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene at 2.54pm after encountering traffic congestion en route.
He said the fire involved three permanent single-storey terrace houses, each measuring about 20ft by 70ft.
"One of the houses sustained about 80 per cent damage, while the other two suffered about five per cent damage each. A Toyota Lite Ace van was also 80 per cent destroyed in the fire.
"The occupant of the badly damaged house managed to escape before firefighters arrived. The victim suffered first-degree burns to the hand and received initial treatment from the Sungai Ara Community Ambulance," he said in a statement.
Photos and video
footage of the incident
were widely shared online.
John said firefighters brought the blaze under control at 3.23pm and were continuing with overhaul operations.
He said 32 personnel from the Jalan Perak, Bayan Baru, Paya Terubong and Lebuh Pantai fire stations were deployed, with assistance from several volunteer fire brigades.
The operation was also assisted by the police, Tenaga Nasional Berhad and the Sungai Ara Community Ambulance.
The cause of the fire is under investigation.
//ENDS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petros CEO, CFO received confidential Petronas document, court told
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The identities behind two Petros email addresses that received a confidential Petronas document were revealed in court today, with a witness testifying that they belonged to Petros' chief executive officer (CEO) and chief financial officer (CFO).
The disclosure surfaced during the trial of former Petronas manager Mohd Khairul Akmal Mohd Jasni, who is accused of attempting to disclose a confidential company document to Petros.
Petros Human Resource Analyst Nurul Qurratu Aini Laila Junaidi, 40, told the Sessions Court that the company's official email system confirmed the email usernames "azha.jalil" and "janin.girie" belonged to Petros' CFO Mohd Azha Abdul Jalil and CEO Datuk Janin Girie, respectively.
It was previously revealed during the trial that Khairul Akmal had sent a confidential document titled "Q1 2024 Upstream Business Performance" from his Petronas email account to two Petros email addresses at 3.19pm on June 8, 2024.
Qurratu Aini, the prosecution's third witness, also said that Khairul Akmal had applied for a senior position at Petros several months before the alleged disclosure.
She said Khairul Akmal applied for the position of Head Upstream Finance through Petros' online recruitment system on Jan 24, 2024.
On March 27, 2024, she said Petros contacted him to offer the position of Head Finance (Gas Aggregator & Sarawak Resource Management), with a monthly salary of RM25,000.
The witness said the offer was made after the interview panel selected Mohd Khairul Akmal as a suitable candidate as he met the criteria required for the position.
"The scope of duties for the position was to manage financial matters for SRM and Gas Aggregator," she said.
However, she said Khairul Akmal requested a higher salary of RM37,000 a month, citing his experience in domestic and international oil and gas operations.
"Petros was unable to meet the request and maintained its original offer of RM25,000 per month.
"Petros did not make any counter-offer after he rejected the offer," she said.
Qurratu Aini also told the court that the interview panel which selected Khairul Akmal comprised Mohd Azha and Petros senior vice-president of Sarawak Resource Management Datuk Abang Arabi Abang Narudin.
Responding to questions from Khairul Akmal's lawyer Louis Liaw Vern Xien, the witness said Petros had never requested confidential information from job candidates during interviews.
Mohd Khairul Akmal, 41, is charged with attempting to disclose the said document to Petros, which he allegedly obtained while carrying out his duties as Petronas' Business Unit Performance Manager.
The offence was allegedly committed at a condominium unit in Jalan Pinang here between 3.19pm and 3.21pm on June 8, 2024.
He faces a charge under Section 203A(1) of the Penal Code, read together with Section 511 of the same law, which carries a maximum fine of RM1 million or imprisonment of up to one year upon conviction.
The trial before Judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid continues tomorrow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Semporna villagers remove 827kg of waste from village, waters
Article:
SEMPORNA: The villagers here have removed 827.3kg of waste from their village and surrounding waters through a recent community-led clean-up.
The effort was part of a pilot community-based waste management system implemented by Reef Check Malaysia with the Kampung Sri Aman Village Committee.
Funded by Etika Sdn Bhd and supported by the Semporna District Council and the state Tourism, Culture and Environment Ministry, the initiative involved 53 community members, including men, women and children.
Residents collected 476.5kg of waste from public areas, mainly plastic packaging and bottles.
The team also worked with waste management company AMWIL, which deployed boats to collect floating debris from surrounding waters, removing another 350.8kg of waste comprising discarded cloth, rubber mats and other bulky materials.
Village head Hassan Gabra Hajan said the community planned to conduct the gotong-royong monthly to complement the existing waste collection system.
"Waste management is not simply about collecting rubbish; it is about building community ownership, changing behaviour and creating systems that local people can sustain themselves.
"Kampung Sri Aman is demonstrating that when communities are empowered with the right support and leadership, they can become the driving force behind solving long-standing issues such as waste management," he said in a statement.
Reef Check Malaysia said the initiative showed how partnerships between communities, local authorities, government agencies and the private sector could help reduce waste leakage into the ocean and strengthen local stewardship.
The organisation hopes the model can inspire similar community-led waste management efforts in coastal villages across Semporna and Sabah.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM5,000 to wed across the border: Why some Malaysians head to Thailand
Article:
KOTA BARU: Malaysian men seeking to take a second wife are willing to pay up to RM5,000 to marriage agents operating along the Malaysia-Thailand border to have their unions solemnised quickly.
The trend was revealed by veteran Thai imam Haji Yusof, 74, who has solemnised marriages involving Malaysian couples for more than two decades.
He said the agents, mostly Thai nationals, arranged the entire cross-border marriage process, from transporting couples into southern Thailand to securing the required religious documents.
"The agents usually bring Malaysian couples across the Golok River into Thailand through Tak Bai or Sungai Golok before taking them either to the Narathiwat Islamic Religious Council office or directly to a mosque in Songkhla for the solemnisation.
"Nowadays, many Malaysians prefer Songkhla because it is closer to the Malaysian Consulate, making it easier to process the required documents afterwards," he told the New Straits Times.
Yusof said Malaysian couples rarely chose mosques in Tak Bai or Sungai Golok, instead preferring agents who could expedite the process.
"Those seeking polygamous marriages are especially willing to spend thousands of ringgit, as long as the marriage can be conducted quickly and with minimal hassle," he said.
He said most couples came from Kelantan and Terengganu, although some were from other states.
Apart from polygamous unions, Yusof said some first-time couples also chose to marry in Thailand because they were unable to obtain parental consent.
He said southern Thailand remained a preferred destination due to its proximity to Malaysia and the availability of agents offering end-to-end services.
Yusof said he had witnessed thousands of marriages involving Malaysians during his more than 20 years as an imam, giving him insight into the changing cross-border marriage landscape.
Southern Thailand has long been a destination for Malaysians seeking to marry, particularly those entering polygamous unions or couples unable to meet marriage requirements in Malaysia.
The Narathiwat Islamic Religious Council has appointed 30 authorised marriage officials across the province to solemnise marriages involving foreign couples, including Malaysians.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM980mil debt burden: Anwar warns Felda against past errors
Article:
MARAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged the current Felda administration to learn from past mistakes and uphold good governance through disciplined and orderly management.
He said poor management and abuse of trust by those entrusted with responsibility had forced the government to spend almost RM1 billion, or RM980 million, to settle Felda's annual debt obligations.
"What is it for? To repay debt. Whose debt? Felda's debt. Was it the settlers' fault? No.
"When we examined the situation, we found there was no proper or systematic management. This is exactly why we must not repeat the same mistakes," he said.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Early voting concludes at all 62 centres
Article:
JOHOR BARU:The early voting process for the 16th Johor State Election involving security personnel and their spouses concluded today after all 62 early voting centres closed by 5pm.
The polling centres, which opened at 8am, were closed in stages according to their locations and the number of registered voters, as determined by the Election Commission (EC).
Earlier, EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun told a press conference at Dewan Mahkota, Kem Mahkota in Kluang that 29 early voting centres were closed at noon.
Another five early voting centres closed at 2pm, while the remaining 28 closed at 5pm.
Today's early voting involved 20,607 electors, comprising 8,544 Malaysian Armed Forces personnel and their spouses as well as 12,063 police personnel and their spouses.
As of 3pm, the EC recorded that 19,136 early voters, or 92.86 per cent, had cast their ballots.
The EC has set a target of 96 per cent turnout for the early voting.
All early ballot boxes will be kept at police stations before the vote counting process begins at 5 pm on polling day this Saturday.
On Saturday, 2,727,926 voters are eligible to cast their ballots at 1,140 polling centres across Johor.
A total of 172 candidates are contesting the 56 seats in the Johor State Legislative Assembly.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor State Election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends marginally lower on mild profit-taking
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended marginally lower on Tuesday as investors engaged in mild profit-taking following the recent rebound in the local market, in line with softer performances across regional markets.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 0.60 of a point to 1,682.93 from Monday's close of 1,683.53.
The benchmark index opened 0.11 of a point lower at 1,683.42 and moved between 1,676.94 and 1,684.19 throughout the session.
The market breadth was negative, with losers outpacing gainers 577 to 410, while 573 counters were unchanged, 1,091 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover edged up to 2.69 billion units valued at RM2.06 billion from 2.68 billion units valued at RM1.69 billion on Monday.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said market sentiment remained cautious despite positive cues from overnight Wall Street.
"Today's decline appears to represent normal consolidation rather than a reversal of sentiment. The benchmark index continues to stabilise above its recent lows, suggesting that the short-term recovery remains intact.
"Looking ahead, the market is expected to remain supported by easing geopolitical tensions, moderating energy prices and resilient global risk appetite," he told Bernama.
Among the heavyweights, Maybank slipped two sen to RM10.92, CIMB lost one sen to RM7.60, Public Bank and Tenaga Nasional were flat at RM4.91 and RM14.30, respectively, while IHH Healthcare gained five sen to RM8.38.
On the most active list, ACE Market debutant RNG Tech remained unchanged at 13 sen, Zetrix AI and Tanco inched down half a sen each to 75.5 sen and 18 sen, respectively, and Asdion slid 1.5 sen to half a sen, while Efficient E-Solutions inched up 1.5 sen to 11.5 sen.
Top gainers included Fraser & Neave, which garnered 56 sen to RM29.66, Batu Kawan added 32 sen to RM21.50, Petronas Gas climbed 30 sen to RM17.80, United Plantations advanced 24 sen to RM33.74, and Hong Leong Bank rose 16 sen to RM21.96.
Of the top losers, Allianz Malaysia fell 50 sen to RM20.60, Dutch Lady Milk dropped 40 sen to RM32, Vitrox trimmed 15 sen to RM7.48, PPB slipped 14 sen to RM9.36, while ITMax shed 12 sen to RM4.76.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index slid 9.93 points to 12,462.79, the FBMT 100 Index fell 8.73 points to 12,292.91, the FBM Emas Shariah Index declined 3.27 points to 12,284.35, the FBM ACE Index decreased 18.72 points to 4,874.15, and the FBM 70 Index dipped 31.04 points to 17,774.60.
By sector, the Energy Index put on 1.84 points to 761.33, the Plantation Index advanced 34.09 points to 9,041.88, while the Financial Services Index shed 6.46 points to 19,927.96, and the Industrial Products and Services Index eased 0.16 of a point to 181.20.
The Main Market volume rose to 1.20 billion units valued at RM1.76 billion compared with 1.06 billion units valued at RM1.36 billion on Monday.
Warrants turnover dropped to 940.84 billion units worth RM136.91 million versus 1.04 billion units worth RM141.68 million previously.
The ACE Market volume declined to 552.24 million units valued at RM156.24 million from 571.10 million units valued at RM193.68 million yesterday.
Consumer products and services counters accounted for 201.43 million shares traded on the Main Market, industrial products and services (230.47 million), construction (78.28 million), technology (218.11 million), financial services (48.44 million), property (176.76 million), plantation (26.67 million), real estate investment trusts (16.20 million), closed-end fund (1.6 million), energy (60.82 million), healthcare (70.59 million), telecommunications and media (21.02 million), transportation and logistics (25.15 million), utilities (20.24 million), and business trusts (105,200).
Meanwhile, Bursa Malaysia said trading in the shares of Enest Group Bhd will be suspended effective 9am, Friday, July 10, 2026.
The exchange in a filing today said Enest's entire issued shares will be removed from the LEAP Market effective 9am Wednesday, July 15, 2026, to facilitate its transfer to the ACE Market.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Policy decisions made via consensus, not dictated by DAP
Article:
SEREMBAN: Government decisions at both the federal and state levels are made collectively through consensus among all component parties rather than dictated by any single faction.
DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke dismissed claims that the administration operates under the thumb of DAP, labelling it an outdated narrative designed to undermine Pakatan Harapan (PH).
"In the federal government, all parties, including DAP, Umno, and PKR, present their views before decisions are finalised by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim," he told reporters here today.
"As component partners and administration members, we naturally express our opinions — we cannot simply sit in silence. Umno and PKR give their views as well, but ultimately, the prime minister makes the final consideration based on the collective interest."
Loke, who is also Negri Sembilan DAP chairman, was asked to comment on allegations that PH blindly follows the dictates of his party in administrative matters.
The transport minister noted that the same collaborative approach is practised in Negri Sembilan, where Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun consults all component parties before making decisions.
He also strongly refuted claims that the rights of the Malay community are under threat in Negri Sembilan, calling it an old political tactic.
"What is being threatened? In Negri Sembilan, everything is fine. The Menteri Besar remains a Malay, and government policies and programmes designed to protect the welfare of all citizens, including the Malays, have been steadily executed since PH took administration of the state in 2018," Loke added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Drug syndicate crippled after Perlis raids uncover RM34.31 million haul
Article:
PADANG BESAR: Police have crippled a drug trafficking syndicate in Perlis following the arrest of three people and the seizure of liquid drugs worth RM34.31 million.
Perlis police chief Datuk Muhammad Abdul Halim said the suspects, comprising a Thai woman and two local men aged between 38 and 68, were arrested in three separate raids across the state between July 1 and July 3.
In the first raid on July 1, a narcotics team detained the 38-year-old Thai woman at Kampung Panggas here.
"A search of a storage facility led to the discovery of 172 boxes containing 75,540 cartridges filled with 188.85 litres of suspected Furanylfentanyl, worth an estimated RM28.33 million.
"Following her arrest, police picked up a 68-year-old local man at Kampung Guar Musang in Bukit Keteri the next day," he told a press conference at the Padang Besar district police headquarters today.
Muhammad said police seized 35 boxes containing 21,000 liquid drug cartridges weighing 39.9 litres, valued at RM5.9 million, during the second raid.
The third suspect, a 55-year-old local man of Thai descent, was arrested on July 3 in Ulu Pauh, Arau.
"Investigations revealed that the syndicate used storage units and residential houses as transit points before smuggling the drugs into a neighbouring country.
"The total haul of 228.75 litres could have fed the habits of 457,500 drug users," he said.
Urine tests showed the third suspect tested positive for tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), while the first two suspects tested negative.
Both local male suspects have prior offences under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and the Poisons Act 1952.
The case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, which carries the death penalty or life imprisonment and whipping upon conviction.
Muhammad added that police are currently tracking down another suspect who remains at large.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia revamps auto incentives, prioritises high-value investments and strengthen SMEs [WATCH]
Article:
RAWANG, Selangor: Malaysia is refining its New Customised Incentive Mechanism (NCIM) to attract higher-quality automotive investments, strengthen domestic industrial capabilities and accelerate the growth of local small and medium enterprises (SMEs).
Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said the enhanced incentive framework forms part of the government's broader strategy to position Malaysia as a manufacturing and export gateway to the Asean market of more than 700 million people.
The revamped NCIM will be simplified to improve clarity for investors while prioritising high-value activities such as engineering, software development, electronics and battery technologies.
Johari said Malaysia's automotive strategy extends beyond vehicle production and focuses on building a resilient manufacturing ecosystem.
The government's long-standing commitment to developing the automotive industry was never solely about vehicle production, he said at the launch of the MCE Auto Hub here today.
It is also about building a robust manufacturing ecosystem that creates quality jobs, nurtures local suppliers and expands the country's automotive component manufacturing capabilities, he added.
Johari said Malaysia's domestic market of about 34 million people should be viewed alongside its strategic position within Asean.
"Malaysia will also continue to leverage its position within Asean as a key competitive advantage for investors, positioning the country as a manufacturing and export hub for the regional market of more than 700 million consumers."
Johari said the government is also refining the methodology for calculating local content while introducing a simpler and more transparent incentive framework to attract quality investments.
"The government hopes to implement the initiative as early as next year once the policy enhancement process has been finalised," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt channels over RM12mil to empower Indian community
Article:
SEREMBAN: The government through the Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra) has allocated more than RM12 million to empower the Indian community through the Early Education Subsidy Assistance Programme (Celik Madani) 2026 and the Third Series of Dharma Madani Programme.
Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said that of the total, RM8.87 million was approved under the Early Education Subsidy Assistance Programme Celik Madani 2026 involving 162 kindergartens nationwide and benefiting 3,612 Indian children from B40 families.
Meanwhile, RM3.36 million was approved for the Third Series of Dharma Madani Programme to 168 Hindu houses of worship nationwide.
"The allocation reflects the government's commitment to strengthening the development of the Indian community through the empowerment of community institutions, expanding access to early education and inclusive human capital development.
"With the disbursement of this third series, the total allocation approved under the Dharma Madani Programme has reached RM12.54 million, involving 627 Hindu houses of worship nationwide. Each temple receives RM20,000 to carry out various programmes with the Indian community, not just for worship," he said.
He said this when officiating the Celik Madani 2026 and Dharma Madani South Zone Programme at Wisma Ceylonese which was also attended by Transport Minister and Seremban Member of Parliament Anthony Loke and Mitra director-general Raveendran Nair.
He said Mitra will continue to ensure that each allocation is channelled transparently and effectively so that more Indian communities can enjoy its benefits, thus creating a knowledgeable, competitive and prosperous community in line with the aspirations of Malaysia Madani.
In the programme, 48 temples and 45 kindergartens from the South Zone (Melaka, Negri Sembilan and Johor) received their respective allocations totalling almost RM3 million through the Dharma Madani and Celik Madani programmes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Padi, rice law revamp: Fines to rise up to RM500,000
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Proposed amendments to the Control of Padi and Rice Act 1994 will significantly increase penalties for offences while expanding regulatory powers over rice grading, pricing and the movement of padi and rice.
The Control of Padi and Rice (Amendment) Bill 2026 was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today.
Under the proposed amendments, fines under Section 22(1) will increase from RM15,000 to RM250,000 for individuals and officers of corporate bodies.
For corporate bodies, the maximum fine under Section 22(2) will rise from RM25,000 to RM500,000.
The Bill also seeks to amend Section 29 to empower the minister to regulate the licensing of padi buyers, determine rice grades and impose price controls on graded rice.
It will also allow the ministry to oversee licensing matters involving padi purchasers.
The Act currently makes it an offence to operate without a required licence, breach licence conditions or fail to comply with regulations issued under the law.
Other offences include violating price controls, conducting unauthorised transfers or distribution of padi and rice, and obstructing enforcement officers carrying out their duties.
The legislation governs the production, purchase, sale, distribution and supply of padi and rice, while regulating industry players through licensing requirements.
It also aims to safeguard the stability of Malaysia's padi and rice supply chain.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rohingya man caught driving without licence in RTD operation
Article:
ALOR STAR: A 32-year-old Rohingya man was arrested after he was caught driving a multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) without a valid licence during a Kedah Road Transport Department (RTD) enforcement operation last night.
Kedah RTD director Stien Van Lutam said the suspect, who works as a construction worker and also operates a grocery store, was detained at about 11pm during Ops PeWa, following public complaints and intelligence gathered by the department.
"The vehicle is believed to have been purchased from a local, who will also face legal action.
"We have issued a summons and seized the vehicle for further investigation, while the registered owner will also be charged in court," he said today.
Stien said the suspect told investigators he was on his way home after closing his grocery store when he was stopped by enforcement officers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Post mortem shows Durian Tunggal victims were executed, say lawyers
Article:
PETALING JAYA: The families of the three men killed in a police operation in Durian Tunggal, Melaka, last November have alleged that post-mortem reports contradicted the police's claim that the officers acted in self-defence.
With fresh evidence in the post mortem reports from Melaka Hospital, the families are demanding an explanation why the policemen involved in the shooting have yet to be arrested and charged.
Lawyer Rajesh Nagarajan who is representing the families said the post-mortem reports have made it clear that this was an execution-style killing.
"They were made to kneel before they were shot. There was no alleged attacks on the policemen," Rajesh said in a Press conference today.
He said the reports revealed downward bullet trajectories and evidence of close-range gunfire, which he said were inconsistent with the official account that the victims were shot after allegedly attacking the police personnel.
"There are no more excuses for the police not to arrest and charge them. We just want justice."
The three victims — T. Pooneswaran, 24, M. Puspanathan, 21 and M. Logeswaran, 29 — were killed during a police operation in Durian Tunggal on Nov 24, 2025.
Police have maintained that the men were shot after allegedly attempting to attack officers with parang along the North-South Expressway, forcing police to open fire in self-defence.
It was later discovered that Logeswaran's wife had been on a phone call with him even before he was stopped by police and inadvertently recorded the incident, until the final gunshots.
Rajesh said the audio recording showed that none of the three men had provoked the officers, with one of them allegedly heard pleading not to be killed before the final shot was heard.
Melaka police had then claimed the three were Durian Tunggal gang robbers who were on the way to commit a burglary in the area when they were intercepted by police.
Rajesh said the post-mortem findings reinforced the families' claims, which were also supported by the audio recording obtained during the incident.
Reading from one of the post-mortem reports, he highlighted the finding that the bullet's path was "from front to back, downwards and to the left", adding that the report also recorded "powder stippling" and "soot soiling" around the gunshot wound.
He said the downward trajectory suggested the victim was in a significantly lower position when shot, while the presence of soot indicated the firearm had been discharged from extremely close range.
"The police are saying that this is self-defence. The post-mortem reports show otherwise.
"It is very clear from all three post-mortem reports that the angle of the shooting and the trajectory are from top to bottom, indicating a shot fired at someone kneeling down," he said, adding the same forensic characteristics were present in all three reports.
Rajesh said that his interpretation was based on findings documented in the Melaka Hospital post-mortem reports.
He also claimed the police and the Attorney-General's Chambers (AGC) had been in possession of the reports for several months before the families obtained copies only a few days ago, questioning why no arrests had been made despite the findings.
"We only got a copy of the post-mortem report two or three days ago. Despite this clear evidence, the police have not arrested anyone, have not remanded anyone and have not charged anyone.
"All that they have been saying is, 'Kita sedang siasat' (We are investigating)."
Rajesh also pointed to what he described as another crucial forensic finding contained in all three post-mortem reports — the presence of "powder stippling" around the entry wounds and "soot soiling" within the wounds.
Quoting directly from the report, he said: "Powder stippling was noted around the wound. Soot soiling was seen on the inner aspect of the wound."
He said soot deposits are typically associated with close-range gunfire.
The lawyer pointed out that more than seven months had elapsed since the killings.
"No one was arrested, no one was remanded, which is completely illogical.
"It does not make sense at all for the police to investigate the police, which is what is happening right now," he said.
Activist Arun Dorasamy, who was also present at the press conference, said the families had fully cooperated with investigators by providing intelligence, enhanced audio recordings, frame-by-frame analysis and information they claimed could identify officers allegedly involved in the incident.
"We personally, together with the families, spent more than 30 to 40 hours with the police and shared all the intelligence that we had," he said.
Arun said the latest forensic findings reinforced evidence the families had previously submitted to investigators.
Arun said Cabinet Minister Gobind Singh Deo had sought answers from the AGC, while Members of Parliament Datuk Seri M. Saravanan, Law Reforms deputy minister M. Kulasegaran and RSN Rayer had repeatedly raised the matter in Parliament.
"Unfortunately, responses from the Home Ministry had consistently been that investigations were still ongoing, but for how long?" he asked.
Conveying the families' demands, lawyer Sachpreetraj Singh Sohanpal said the victims' families want the Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail and the Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar to explain the delay in the investigation and prosecution.
The New Straits Times has contacted Mohd Khalid, Mohd Dusuki and Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar and are awaiting their response.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Health Ministry records 73 heat-related illness cases, four deaths since Jan 1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 73 heat-related illness cases have been recorded nationwide since Jan 1, according to the Health Ministry's Disease Control Division.
Public Health Medicine Specialist Dr Hazlina Yahaya said the cases comprised 59 instances of heat exhaustion, six of exertional heat stroke, four of classical heat stroke, two of postpartum hyperpyrexia, and two of heat cramps.
"The cumulative death toll remains at four cases, involving two adult males aged 42 in Penang and 22 in Kedah, as well as two children aged two in Negri Sembilan and four in Perak," she said.
Dr Hazlina presented the data during a media engagement session on government preparedness for the 2026 Southwest Monsoon today.
She said public healthcare facilities are fully equipped with manpower, treatment facilities, and medical supplies to ensure prompt care.
The ministry has also intensified health education, advocacy programmes, and public information campaigns, particularly for high-risk groups like the Orang Asli community.
"The ministry is also carrying out advocacy efforts to eliminate mosquito breeding sites and to encourage early treatment for those experiencing symptoms," Dr Hazlina added.
She said the ministry will continue monitoring the situation, strengthen risk communication, and enhance healthcare system preparedness through targeted surveillance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia's 2026 budget deficit seen widening to 2.85pct of GDP
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's budget deficit is expected to widen to 2.85 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP) this year, bigger than originally predicted, the government said on Tuesday, even as it aims to scale back spending on the president's flagship school meals programme.
The new estimate is close to the legislated deficit ceiling of three per cent of GDP.
It is also wider than both the government's previous estimate of a deficit of 2.68 per cent this year and the 2025 budget gap of 2.81 per cent of GDP.
Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa presented the full-year outlook at a hearing with parliament's budget commission, based on figures showing the deficit in the first half was 196.5 trillion rupiah (US$10.9 billion), equivalent to 0.76 per cent of GDP.
Indonesia's government typically spends more towards the end of a fiscal year because payments for some programmes are authorised after audits.
For example, in the first half of last year the budget deficit was 0.84 per cent of GDP. The budget has faced pressure this year from higher energy subsidy costs due to the Iran war and capital outflows amid a global "risk-off" sentiment, including concerns over the high cost of the free meals programme.
Purbaya said total revenues were expected to be 3,208.1 trillion rupiah this year, 1.7 per cent above the government's original target as income has been boosted by high commodity prices.
But spending is expected to be 2.6 per cent more than previously expected at 3,942.4 trillion rupiah, which the finance ministry attributed in part to an additional allocation of 132 trillion rupiah for energy subsidies in response to the global surge in prices.
Said Abdullah, the head of the house's budget panel, said the total spending estimate included a 67 trillion rupiah cut in the budget for the free meals programme to 268 trillion rupiah, but did not incorporate potential further cuts because the government and parliament are still discussing the size of them.
Purbaya has previously said a further cut of 40 trillion rupiah is possible as part of an efficiency drive, but Abdullah said the budget panel was looking for double that.
The rupiah showed little reaction to the forecast of a wider deficit, trading around 17,980 per dollar in the afternoon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police call for stronger school, family support after Banting stabbing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have called for closer coordination between schools, families and mental health professionals to strengthen intervention efforts and reduce the risk of violent incidents involving students.
The call follows the arrest of a
teenage suspect
after a 15-year-old secondary school student was stabbed in Banting, with the case being investigated under Section 307 of the Penal Code for attempted murder.
Federal police Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department director Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin said while such incidents could not be completely prevented, early identification, consistent treatment and stronger support systems could help reduce risks.
He said prevention required more than addressing a student's mental health or behavioural issues, with timely communication between parents, schools and medical professionals crucial for effective intervention.
"Known cases that are receiving treatment have a much higher chance of prevention. The biggest challenge is not the treatment itself, but the system around," he said when contacted.
Noor Hisam said consistent care, including therapy, medication and follow-up sessions with psychiatrists or psychologists, could help stabilise symptoms and reduce the likelihood of impulsive or aggressive behaviour.
He said schools should strengthen counselling services, monitor behavioural changes and establish safety plans, especially when teachers and counsellors identify potential triggers.
He said that factors such as bullying, family problems, social media influence and access to dangerous items could also contribute to risks.
"Parents, doctors and schools need to communicate. When families are alert to changes in medication, stress levels or early warning signs, action can be taken before the situation becomes serious," he added.
Asked whether police were reviewing school security measures following the incident, Noor Hisam did not comment on perimeter controls or entry protocols.
However, he said stigma, privacy concerns and limited resources remained challenges in prevention efforts.
He said that having a mental health condition did not mean a person was likely to become violent.
"Not everyone with depression, anxiety or behavioural disorders becomes violent. The majority do not.
"This makes it difficult to predict who is truly at high risk without unfairly labelling everyone," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SkyeChip advances interface IP engagement with Cerebras
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: SkyeChip Bhd is engaging a global artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure company Cerebras Systems Inc in advancing ultra-fast AI computing through its industry-leading wafer-scale engine technology.
As part of the engagement, the integrated circuit company is developing custom high-speed interface intellectual property (IP) for US-based Cerebras' next-generation computing initiatives.
SkyeChip said the engagement involves milestone-based development, rigorous technical validation and close collaboration across the semiconductor ecosystem.
It said these efforts are helping to develop production-ready interface IP that meets the performance and reliability requirements of next-generation AI computing.
"This engagement reflects SkyeChip's continued growth in supporting advanced, performance-critical semiconductor programs through our interface IP and engineering capabilities," its chief executive officer Datuk SK Fong said.
Cerebras vice president David Greenhill said SkyeChip's technical expertise and extensive experience in designing complex IP were key factors in the company's decision to collaborate with the Malaysian firm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah looks to develop local talent for semiconductor industry
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah government is considering training more local talent to support the semiconductor industry at the new Kota Belud Industrial Park.
Sabah Industrial Development, Entrepreneurship and Transport permanent secretary Datuk Thomas Logijin said the semiconductor ecosystem generates significant value-added activities and has the potential to substantially increase Sabah's gross domestic product (GDP).
"However, during our recent visit to Taiwan with Kota Kinabalu Industrial Park, one message became clear: without talent, there is no way we can build a semiconductor industry.
"KKIP is now working with a Malaysian company based in Taiwan to identify and train local talent for this industry.
"Beyond semiconductor design, Sabah will also require many engineers, researchers and technical specialists," he said during a forum titled "Driving SMJ 2.0: Accelerating the Three Core Sectors for Sabah's RM100 Billion GDP Target", held in conjunction with Sankalpa Sabah 2030 at a conference venue here.
At present, Sabah has four established industrial parks: Kota Kinabalu Industrial Park (KKIP), Sabah Agro-Industrial Precinct, Sandakan Industrial Park, and Sipitang Oil and Gas Industrial Park.
The Kota Belud and Kudat industrial parks are the latest to be approved by the Sabah Cabinet, with KKIP appointed as the coordinator for the Kota Belud Industrial Park.
Thomas said the ministry aimed to introduce a true plug-and-play concept at the two new industrial parks, allowing investors to begin operations with all the necessary infrastructure, utilities, connectivity and support services already in place.
Apart from talent development and attracting local and foreign investment, he said downstream industries could generate greater economic value and create more high-paying jobs for Sabahans.
He cited solar glass manufactured from silica sand sourced in Kudat, which could be further processed into solar panels, while biomass from the palm oil industry could be developed into renewable energy products.
"If we establish the entire manufacturing ecosystem here, we will create high-quality jobs for future generations.
"Now, a fresh graduate entering the civil service under N41 grade (entry level) may earn just over RM2,000 per month.
"Workers in advanced manufacturing industries, however, can begin with salaries of around RM4,000, or roughly two-thirds of what similar workers earn in Singapore."
Last year, the manufacturing sector contributed about 7.2 per cent to Sabah's RM88.8 billion GDP.
"To achieve a RM100 billion GDP, the manufacturing sector must play a much more significant role.
"The vision of our ministry is to transform Sabah into an industrial state by 2040. It means manufacturing should contribute at least 25 per cent of Sabah's GDP.
"We are hoping in 10 years from now that the ministry can contribute RM40 billion to our GDP."
Thomas said he was optimistic the target could be achieved with several key infrastructure projects, including the completion of the Sabah Port expansion next year, which will increase its handling capacity to 1.25 million from the current 400 Twenty-foot Equivalent Units.
Others include additional oil and gas discoveries by Petronas to strengthen Sabah's long-term energy security, and the expected completion of the Pan Borneo Highway within the next two to three years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar unveils seven incentives for Felda settlers, including new generation housing
Article:
MARAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced seven incentives for Felda settlers covering housing, education, and healthcare initiatives.
Speaking at the national-level 2026 Felda Settlers' Day celebration and Felda's 70th anniversary, Anwar said the government will establish a dedicated committee to resolve long-standing issues involving second-generation Felda settlers.
"The government fully understands the concerns of the new generation of Felda settlers regarding housing, particularly issues related to the concept of building two houses on a single lot (2R1L). We have identified key strategic initiatives to address the new-generation housing issue.
"The initiatives include proposed amendments to the Group Settlement Areas Act 1960 to allow more than one housing unit to be built on a single residential lot.
"This proposal is currently being finalised and will be tabled in Parliament this year," he said at the Tun Abdul Razak Stadium in Jengka today.
Anwar said 8,000 2R1L housing units had been completed and occupied as of Dec 31 last year, adding that the government would approve water and electricity utility connections based on applications received.
"For the construction of new units after that date, I urge everyone to be patient. Allow time for the proposed amendments to be finalised to ensure that land ownership rights in Felda schemes are managed in a more orderly and well-planned manner," he said.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Maybank Islamic launches 14pct fixed-rate Syariah-compliant credit card
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Maybank Islamic Bhd has introduced a Syariah-compliant credit card offering a fixed profit rate of 14 per cent per annum, as the bank looks to provide a simpler and more predictable financing option for consumers.
The Nadi Mastercard Credit Card-i carries no annual fee and does not impose compounding charges; it is aimed at customers seeking straightforward access to credit with greater certainty over financing costs.
"The launch comes as more Malaysians seek practical and affordable financial solutions that are easy to understand and aligned with their needs and values.
"Nadi Mastercard Credit Card-i reflects this shift by making access to credit more straightforward, predictable and accessible," it said in a statement.
The bank said cardholders will have access to Maybank's wider card ecosystem, including more than 1,000 merchant privileges across the region.
Maybank president and group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Khairussaleh Ramli said the card aims to expand access to responsible financing by offering a solution that is affordable, accessible and relevant to customers' daily needs.
"We remain committed to shaping a future of banking that delivers sustainable impact for the communities we serve," he said.
The card will be available for application through the MAE app, Maybank2u and Maybank branches nationwide from October 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar says Putrajaya mulling Land Act amendment for multiple houses on Felda lots
Article:
MARAN, July 7 — The government plans to amend the Land (Group Settlement Areas) Act 1960 (Act 530) to allow more than one house to be built on a single residential lot owned by Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) settlers, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said he had instructed Felda to prepare draft amendments to the Act within two months for submission to the Cabinet before they are tabled in Parliament later this year.
“At present, about 8,000 houses have already been built on individual lots and have been occupied since Dec 31, 2025. While the legal amendments are being finalised, we will grant immediate approval for water and electricity connections to these homes,” he said at the Felda Settlers’ Day and Felda’s 70th Anniversary celebrations at Tun Abdul Razak Stadium in Bandar Pusat Jengka today.
The prime minister, who is also finance minister, said responsibility for water supply would be handed to the respective state governments, while Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB) had been instructed to expedite electricity connections to all 8,000 houses.
Meanwhile, Anwar said the Felda New Generation Housing Project (PGBF), introduced in 2013, covers 43 sites involving a total of 8,224 housing units across seven states, namely Pahang, Johor, Negeri Sembilan, Kedah, Terengganu, Kelantan and Perak. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia schools need proactive safety policy, not reactive responses, says official
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The government has been urged to draw up a National School Safety Master Plan as a comprehensive policy to strengthen the protection of students following several incidents of violence in schools that have sparked public concern in recent times.
Federal Territories State Leadership Council Education Bureau chairman Zaleha Dullah said the master plan should encompass physical security, risk management, emergency procedures and a standardised monitoring mechanism for implementation across all schools.
She said the plan could be formulated through a National School Safety Roundtable involving the Ministry of Education (MOE), security agencies, psychologists, academics, parents’ associations, civil society organisations and student representatives.
Expressing sympathy to the victim and the family, she said recent incidents of school violence should serve as a reminder that protection systems and early intervention measures in schools must be strengthened to prevent similar occurrences.
“It is time for a more comprehensive approach to be implemented. We can no longer afford to be reactive by acting only after a tragedy has occurred. The country needs a more proactive policy in addressing issues of violence, bullying, mental health and student safety,” she said in a statement today.
Zaleha proposed increasing the number of guidance and counselling teachers, professional counsellors and educational psychologists to ensure students experiencing emotional distress or displaying behavioural changes could be identified early and receive appropriate assistance.
She also called for regular psychosocial screening, enhanced security controls at school entrances based on risk assessments, as well as closer cooperation with the authorities to strengthen safety measures in schools.
In addition, she proposed strengthening character education, emotional management, conflict resolution and digital literacy, while raising parents’ awareness of the importance of monitoring their children’s use of social media, video games and digital content.
She said schools, parents, communities, the police, psychologists and relevant agencies should work more closely to establish a more comprehensive support system for students.
“Every child sent to school is a responsibility entrusted to the nation. Parents send their children to school with the hope that they will return home with knowledge, not tragedy. Student safety must therefore be the foremost priority in every national education policy,” she added. — Bernama

Title: Johor election: KTMB ramps up ETS services with 20pc discount
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) is ramping up its Electric Train Service (ETS) capacity this weekend to accommodate a surge of passengers heading south for the Johor state election this Saturday.
Following a total sell-out of an earlier batch of tickets on June 19, KTMB is deploying eight additional services across two key routes: KL Sentral–JB Sentral–KL Sentral and JB Sentral–Gemas–JB Sentral.
For those planning their trip, ticket sales for the JB Sentral–Gemas–JB Sentral route opened today at 3:00 pm. Tickets for the KL Sentral–JB Sentral–KL Sentral route will go on sale Wednesday at 9:00 am.
Operating from July 10 to 12, these extra trips offer 312 seats each, adding a total of 7,464 tickets over the three-day period. The move is designed to give Johor voters more flexibility and a more comfortable journey home.
To make the trip easier on the pocket, KTMB is offering a 20 per cent promotional discount on these additional ETS services from July 10 to 12. The discount aligns with the government’s “Road to Rail” initiative, encouraging Malaysians to swap the steering wheel for a train seat.
“This initiative is expected to encourage more members of the public to choose rail transport to return and fulfil their responsibility as voters, thereby contributing to smoother traffic flow and supporting the nation's sustainable mobility agenda,” KTMB said.
Passengers are advised to book early to secure affordable fares and avoid the last-minute rush. To ensure a seamless boarding process, travellers should arrive at the station at least 30 minutes before departure, keeping in mind that boarding gates close strictly five minutes before the train leaves.
Tickets are available via the KITS Style app, the official
KTMB website
, or ticket kiosks at selected stations.

Title: Corporate espionage case: Ex-Petronas manager sent upstream performance report to Petros email, witness says
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The Sessions Court was told today that Mohd Khairul Akmal Mohd Jasni, a former Petronas manager, had applied to work at Petroleum Sarawak Berhad (Petros) months before he allegedly leaked the national oil company’s financial and business secrets to the same company.
Petros Human Resource Analyst Nurul Qurratu Aini Laila Junaidi, 40, testified that Mohd Khairul Akmal had submitted an application for the Head Upstream Finance position through its website’s internal system on Jan 24, 2024.
“On March 27, 2024, I contacted Mohd Khairul Akmal via email and telephone to extend a job offer for the position of Head Finance (Gas Aggregator & Sarawak Resource Management (SRM)), with a monthly salary of RM25,000. The scope of duties for the position involved managing financial affairs for SRM and Gas Aggregator,” she said.
She was testifying during the trial of Mohd Khairul Akmal, who faces charges of attempting to disclose the said financial and business secrets to Petros in June 2024.
The third prosecution witness stated that the job offer was made after the interview panel selected Mohd Khairul Akmal as the suitable candidate for the position, as the accused met all the criteria established for the role.
Nurul Qurratu Aini Laila testified that during the process, Mohd Khairul Akmal had requested a salary of RM37,000 per month, but Petros was unable to meet that demand and stood by its original offer of RM25,000.
“The reason he requested a higher salary than Petros’s initial offer was due to his experience in both domestic and international oil and gas operations. After he rejected the offer, Petros did not make any counter-offer,” the witness said.
Separately, Nurul Qurratu Aini Laila said that she knew Datuk Janin Girie as the chief executive officer and Mohd Azha Abdul Jalil as the chief financial officer of Petros, and was aware of their email addresses based on information in the company’s official email system.
On June 25 last year, the first prosecution witness, former Petronas senior manager of Industrial Relations Jumsuri Basri, 48, testified that a review revealed that on June 8, 2024, at 3.19 pm, Mohd Khairul Akmal had sent a confidential document titled “Q1 2024 Upstream Business Performance” via his Petronas email to a Petros email address.
The following day, the second prosecution witness, Petronas general manager Syakirah Mustapa, 51, told the court that Mohd Khairul Akmal had never been permitted to use the report for personal purposes or to share it with third parties, namely Janin and Mohd Azha.
Meanwhile, under cross-examination by Mohd Khairul Akmal’s counsel Louis Liaw Vern Xien, the witness confirmed that the interview panel which selected him as the suitable candidate for the position of Head Finance (Gas Aggregator & SRM) consisted of Mohd Azha and SRM senior vice president Datuk Abang Arabi Abang Narudin.
When asked by Liaw whether, throughout her time with the company, Petros had ever requested confidential information from job candidates during interviews, Nurul Qurratu Aini Laila answered in the negative.
Mohd Khairul Akmal, 41, is charged with attempting to disclose a document titled “Q1 2024 Upstream Business Performance Operational & Financial” to Petros, allegedly obtained during the course of his duties as Petronas Business Unit performance manager, at a condominium unit along Jalan Pinang here, between 3.19 pm and 3.21 pm on June 8, 2024.
He is charged under Section 203A(1) of the Penal Code, read together with Section 511 of the same Code, which provides for a maximum fine of RM1 million or imprisonment of up to one year, while Section 511 carries a penalty of imprisonment not exceeding half of the maximum term provided under Section 203A(1), upon conviction.
The trial before Judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid resumes tomorrow. — Bernama

Title: ‘Preparations made’: Nadma says govt agencies ready for possible ‘Super El Nino’
Article:
PUCHONG, July 7 — Government agencies responsible for disaster management are on high alert ahead of the possible onset of a ‘Super El Nino’ towards the end of the year, while also stepping up preparations to mitigate the risks of extreme heat, haze and open burning during the Southwest Monsoon.
National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) director-general Meor Ismail Meor Akim said the coordination meeting held today was not because agencies were unprepared, but to update the public on their current state of readiness and strengthen coordination among the ministries and agencies involved.
“All agencies have taken the necessary measures. We invited the media today to brief the public on the preparations that have been made so that people are also aware and better prepared.
“The government’s approach is no longer one of waiting for incidents to occur. Instead, the focus is on early action, risk reduction and comprehensive preparedness,” he told a press conference after a media engagement session on the government’s preparedness for the 2026 Southwest Monsoon here today.
The session featured integrated briefings by six agencies — the Health Ministry (MoH), Education Ministry (MoE), the Malaysian Meteorological Department (METMalaysia), the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM), the Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) and the Department of Minerals and Geoscience Malaysia (JMG).
Meor Ismail said all government assets could be mobilised under the national disaster management mechanism to ensure a swift response should the situation become more critical.
He also urged the media to help disseminate information on preventive measures and preparedness so the public would be more aware of the risks posed by hot weather, open burning and haze in the coming months.
Meanwhile, METMalaysia deputy director-general (Operations) Ambun Dindang said developments relating to the possible ‘Super El Nino’ were being closely monitored using data from the United States’ National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) and Climate Prediction Centre (CPC), particularly changes in sea surface temperatures in the central Pacific Ocean.
Based on current climate models, he said the phenomenon is expected to begin developing around November before affecting Malaysia early next year, although the forecast remains subject to change as new data become available.
“The closer we get to the expected period, the more accurate the data will be. At that stage, more targeted measures can be implemented together with the relevant agencies,” he said.
Ambun said the Southwest Monsoon is expected to bring hotter and drier weather, with reduced rainfall in several areas, increasing the risk of hotspots, forest fires, peatland fires and transboundary haze.
“The risk of transboundary haze will depend on the occurrence of fires in neighbouring countries and prevailing wind patterns. METMalaysia will continue monitoring the situation before issuing any projections or appropriate warnings,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Sharing Banting stabbing children’s identities could land you in jail, warns Suhakam
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Malaysia’s human rights commission has warned that sharing photographs, videos or other identifying information about the children involved in the recent Banting school stabbing could be a criminal offence.
In a statement today, the Office of the Children’s Commissioner of the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) urged the public to stop circulating content that could harm both the victim and the alleged attacker.
It said both children were entitled to legal protections under the Child Act 2001 and the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child, regardless of the ongoing police investigation.
Suhakam said it was “deeply concerned” by the widespread circulation of photographs, videos, personal information and unverified claims on social media and messaging platforms.
“Such actions can cause lasting psychological harm, expose children to public stigma, interfere with investigations, and compromise their rehabilitation and future reintegration into society,” it said.
The stabbing occurred at about 9.30am yesterday while the victim was at the school’s canteen.
Police said the suspect, a 15-year-old student at the same school, allegedly entered through the main entrance before stabbing the victim multiple times.
The victim was rushed to Banting Hospital, while the suspect was arrested shortly afterwards.
Kuala Langat police chief Superintendent Mohd Akmalrizal Radzi said the teenager has been remanded for four days until Friday to assist investigations under Section 307 of the Penal Code for attempted murder.
Suhakam reminded the public, media organisations and online users that Section 15 of the Child Act prohibits publishing information that could identify a child involved in protected proceedings or matters, including names, photographs, videos, school details and family information. Offenders face a fine of up to RM10,000, up to five years’ imprisonment, or both.
It also urged the public not to speculate about the child’s medical condition, disability, mental health or alleged motive, warning that doing so could prejudice investigations and perpetuate harmful stereotypes.
